agent.effectiveKnowledgeRule: Knowledge: apply permission filtering first, then the allowed-knowledge whitelist (if configured).

kbConfig.strategyParagraphHint: Cuts by paragraphs. Good for cleanly structured docs, less stable on uneven sections.
kbConfig.strategySemanticHint: Cuts by semantic boundaries. Usually best retrieval quality and recommended by default.
kbConfig.enrichmentMaxTokensHint: Set 0 to follow provider model max output tokens
kbConfig.minRelevanceScoreHint: Higher threshold reduces irrelevant hits; lower threshold improves recall coverage.

memory.title: Memory System
memory.resetNotice: Memory System keeps durable user memory and reviewable skill candidates together. Shared organizational knowledge lives in Knowledge Base.
memory.tabsHint: User Memory stores durable facts and events about a user. Skill Candidates store reusable action patterns distilled from the same memory pipeline and can be reviewed before promotion into the Skill Library.

description.agent: Private memories automatically generated by agents during task execution. Agents use these to learn from past experiences.
description.company: Shared organizational knowledge accessible to all agents. Use this for company policies, processes, and shared insights.
description.userContext: Personal preferences and context about individual users. Agents reference these to provide personalized interactions.
description.taskContext: Task-scoped context memory such as goals, key conclusions, and execution summaries. Prioritized when the related task runs.
description.userMemory: Long-term user facts including relationships, experiences, preferences, goals, and important events.
description.skillProposal: Reviewable skill candidates distilled from the same memory pipeline. After approval, they publish into the Skill Library for reuse.

empty.agent: No agent memories yet. Memories will be automatically created as agents complete tasks.

memory.noResults: No memories match your search criteria.

create.title: New Memory
create.agentBindingHint: This association is for traceability only and does not directly control access. Visibility is managed by publish/share policy (scope + explicit exceptions).
create.companyOnly: Shared knowledge now belongs in the Knowledge Base. User memory and skill candidates are generated automatically from conversations and tasks.
create.defaultVisibilityHint: Newly created memories default to department visibility (`department`). Configure agent-specific, cross-department, or org-wide sharing in Share/Publish after creation.

detail.title: Memory Detail
detail.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this memory?

share.title: Share Memory
share.defaultVisibility: Default visibility: same department; parent-department managers can read child departments.
share.extraShareHint: The list below is only for extra sharing to external users/agents.
share.publishHint: Promote working memory into team/org memory. It is not shared to individual agents directly.
share.shareHint: Configure visibility scope and explicit exceptions. Default access follows department/org policy.

scopeDesc.privateUser: User-profile memory stays private and is read by that user's agents.
scopeDesc.explicit: Only explicitly authorized users can read.
scopeDesc.explicitException: Keep user profile private and add explicit exceptions only when necessary.
scopeDesc.department: Readable by users in the same department.
scopeDesc.departmentTree: Same department + inherited manager read from parent departments.

share.explicitUsersPlaceholder: One user_id per line, or comma-separated

share.explicitNeedsUsers: Explicit scope requires at least one user ID.
share.reasonPlaceholder: Reason for sharing/publishing (used in audit log)
share.reasonRequired: Reason is required when explicit exceptions are configured.
share.noTargets: No agents or users available to share with.
share.reviewReviseSuccess: Skill candidate sent back for revision

card.reviewPendingHint: This skill candidate is waiting for review and has not been promoted into the Skill Library yet.
card.reviewPublishedHint: This skill candidate has been approved and promoted or merged into the Skill Library.
card.reviewRejectedHint: This skill candidate was rejected and will not be promoted into the Skill Library.

retrievalTest.title: Memory Retrieval Test
retrievalTest.queryPlaceholder: Enter query text to test memory recall...
retrievalTest.emptyHint: Enter a query and click Search to test memory retrieval.

config.title: Pipeline Configuration
config.editorSubtitle: Configure user-memory retrieval, rerank, planner, extraction, skill learning, session-ledger cleanup, and runtime context source toggles.
config.loadFailed: Failed to load memory pipeline config
config.embeddingHint: Choose the embedding provider/model used to write and query user-memory vectors. Only embedding-capable providers are listed.
config.inheritEmbedding: Allow fallback to knowledge-base embedding when memory embedding is blank
config.retrievalHint: Configure the live hybrid retrieval pipeline used by user-memory runtime and API search.
config.retrievalEffectiveHint: These controls are live in the current user-memory retrieval path, including rerank, planner, and reflection.
config.similarityThresholdHint: Final floor for memory recall. Lower improves recall; higher improves precision.
config.rerankHint: After semantic, lexical, and structured recall merge, rerank reorders candidates before final filtering.
config.rerankTopKHint: How many merged candidates enter the reranker.
config.rerankWeightHint: How strongly rerank scores influence the final blended score.
config.docMaxCharsHint: Maximum text length sent per candidate to the reranker.
config.rerankProviderHint: Only providers that expose rerank models are listed here.
config.rerankModelHint: Choose a rerank model, not a chat or embedding model.
config.plannerHint: Planner decides how to expand a query before retrieval. Runtime light mode uses rules only; full mode adds one generation-model call to produce query variants and structured filters.
config.plannerExplain: Use light when you want low latency and predictable behavior. Use full when you want stronger recall and can afford one extra chat-model call.
config.plannerModeDisabledHint: Planner provider/model settings are only used when runtime mode or API mode is set to full.
config.runtimeModeHint: Agent runtime should usually stay on light to avoid adding extra model latency.
config.apiModeHint: API search can use full mode when you want better recall and can afford one extra model call.
config.maxQueryVariantsHint: Upper bound for planner-generated search rewrites in full mode.
config.plannerProviderHint: Only providers with chat/generation models are listed here. Embedding and rerank models cannot be used as planner models.
config.plannerModelHint: Choose a chat/generation model that can reliably follow structured instructions.
config.reflectionHint: Optional second-pass expansion used only for API retrieval when first-pass recall looks weak.
config.maxRoundsHint: Usually keep this at 1; higher values add latency quickly.
config.minResultsHint: If first-pass recall returns fewer than this many results, reflection may run.
config.minScoreHint: If the best first-pass result is below this score, reflection may run.
config.runtimeHint: Control which durable sources are injected into the runtime prompt assembler.
config.runtimeEffectiveHint: These three source toggles are wired into the runtime context path.
config.factExtractionHint: Extract stable user facts from completed sessions before writing them into user-memory entries and views.
config.factExtractionModeHint: These controls tune the user-memory fact extractor. Skill-candidate extraction is configured separately below.
config.factExtractionEffectiveHint: Provider/model, timeout, backoff, max user facts, heuristic fallback, and secondary recall all affect the active session-extraction pipeline. Max Facts is the normalization cap used when entries are written.
config.factExtractionProviderHint: Primary provider for session extraction; if unavailable, the system falls back to agent/global model defaults.
config.factExtractionMaxFactsHint: Second-stage storage normalization cap per memory item.
config.factExtractionMaxPreferenceFactsHint: Stage-1 session extraction cap for stable user facts per flush.
config.factExtractionModelHintNew: Primary model for session extraction and storage normalization.
config.factExtractionTimeoutHint: Per-attempt timeout for first-stage session extraction requests.
config.factExtractionBackoffHint: After extraction failures, skip new attempts for this duration.
config.enableHeuristicFallback: Enable heuristic fallback when LLM extraction returns no user facts
config.secondaryRecallEnabled: Run a second explicit-recall pass when the primary extraction misses user facts
config.maintenanceHint: Tune post-write consolidation separately from extraction so user-memory views stay deduplicated and current.
config.sessionLedgerHint: Session ledger keeps short-lived provenance only; durable user-memory entries, views, and skill candidates remain after cleanup.
config.skillCandidatesHint: Configure how successful execution paths become reviewable skill candidates before promotion into the canonical skill library.
config.skillCandidatesEffectiveHint: Candidate extraction settings below are active. Approved candidates can then be promoted or merged into canonical skills.
config.maxSkillCandidatesHint: Caps how many reusable skill candidates the session extractor can keep per flush.
config.autoBindSourceAgent: Auto-bind promoted candidates back to the source agent

userManagement.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
userManagement.confirmDisable: Are you sure you want to disable this user?
userManagement.confirmEnable: Are you sure you want to enable this user?
userManagement.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
userManagement.accessDenied: You do not have permission to access this page

roleManagement.title: Roles & Permissions
roleManagement.subtitle: View system role definitions and permission assignments
roleManagement.infoMessage: Roles and permissions are defined at the system level. Each higher role inherits all permissions from the roles below it. Contact the system administrator if you need role configuration changes.

descriptions.admin: Full system access with all management capabilities including user administration, system configuration, and all resource operations.
descriptions.manager: Department-level management with the ability to manage agents, tasks, knowledge, and view users within their department.
descriptions.user: Standard access for creating and managing own agents, tasks, and knowledge base items. Can collaborate within assigned departments.
descriptions.viewer: Read-only access to view agents, tasks, knowledge, and shared resources. Cannot create or modify any resources.

missions.title: Mission Control
missions.retryFailedPartsHint: Available when mission status is failed or cancelled
missions.retryThisTaskHint: Available when mission status is failed or cancelled
missions.clarificationNeeded: Clarification needed: please reply to continue mission execution
missions.clarificationEmpty: Please enter a clarification response first.
missions.subtitle: Create missions and watch your AI team execute them
missions.deleteConfirm: Delete mission "{{title}}"? Running missions will be cancelled.
missions.createFirst: Create your first mission to get started
missions.settingsDescription: Configure default LLM models and execution parameters for mission roles.
missions.preferExistingAgentsHint: Try existing platform agents first; fallback still applies by policy.
missions.allowTemporaryWorkersHint: Automatically create temporary specialist agents when no suitable existing agent is available.
missions.titlePlaceholder: e.g., Build landing page for product launch
missions.autoGenerateTitleHint: Leave blank to let the Leader generate a title from requirements.
missions.instructionsPlaceholder: Describe what you want the AI team to accomplish...
missions.dropFilesHere: Drop files here or click to browse
missions.fileTypesHint: PDF, DOCX, TXT, images, and more
missions.startFailed: Mission was created, but auto-start failed. Please start it manually.
missions.deliverablesLoadFailed: Failed to load deliverables and workspace files.
missions.intermediateFilesHint: Includes temporary/process files for debugging reference.
missions.finalDeliverablesHint: Only final target deliverables for acceptance and download.
missions.workspaceProcessHint: Shows workspace process files and persisted intermediate artifacts (logs, task drafts, shared materials).
missions.noPlannedTasksYet: Tasks have not been planned yet.
missions.assignmentReasonLeaderNotFound: Planner-selected agent unavailable, fallback policy applied.
missions.assignmentReasonBlueprintAssigned: Team blueprint matched an existing platform agent.
missions.assignmentReasonBlueprintNotFound: Team blueprint preferred agent unavailable, fallback policy applied.
missions.assignmentReasonAutoMatch: Auto matched by capability and context relevance.
missions.assignmentReasonNoExisting: No suitable existing agent found for this task.
missions.assignmentReasonNoAgents: No platform agents available right now.
missions.assignmentReasonTempPolicy: Policy allows temporary workers first for this task.
missions.assignmentReasonTempPending: Temporary worker will be provisioned during execution.
missions.assignmentReasonTempProvisioned: Temporary worker was created and assigned for execution.
missions.assignmentReasonTempDisabled: Temporary workers are disabled and no existing agent matched.
missions.reviewSummaryUnavailable: Task-level review metrics are unavailable for this node.
missions.reviewTimelineHint: Filtered to review/rework events for this task in the latest run.
missions.noReviewAttempts: No review attempts recorded in this run.
missions.noReworkCycles: No rework cycle retried in this run.
missions.maxRetriesHint: Execution retries per attempt chain (used for task run and phase prompt failures).
missions.maxReworkCyclesHint: Review rework loops after a FAIL verdict (execute -> review -> rework -> review).

notificationsPage.title: Notification Center
notificationsPage.subtitle: Process high-volume alerts and historical events with server-side pagination
notificationsPage.confirmClearAll: Clear all notifications? This action cannot be undone.
notificationsPage.localHistoryHint: The server returned no records for current filters. These notifications come from local browser storage.

robots.title: Robotic Workers
robots.subtitle: Manage and coordinate your physical robotic entities
robots.demoNoticeContent: This page is currently for demonstration purposes only. To integrate and manage your physical robots (such as Unitree robot dogs, industrial collaborative arms, etc.) in a real environment, please contact the service provider for customized integration development.

unitree_h2.name: Unitree H2
unitree_h2.description: General humanoid robot with enhanced payload and complex task capabilities.

unitree_a2.name: Unitree A2
unitree_a2.description: High-performance quadruped for autonomous operation and transport in complex terrain.

openclaw_pc.name: OpenClaw Smart Workstation
openclaw_pc.description: Intelligent terminal with high-performance computing for precise office automation.
